pattern: Use the KMP failure function to find all positions p where word[p:] is a prefix of word; the minimum t is the smallest t>=1 such that t*k >= len(word) or t*k is one of those positions.
hints: After t operations only word[t*k:] must match a prefix of the original word (the t*k appended characters can be chosen freely).
A position p is a valid split point if word[p:] equals word[:len(word)-p]; these are exactly the positions stored in the KMP failure/prefix array.

Build the KMP prefix table for the original word; collect all positions p where the failure link chain reaches the full-match state, then find the smallest t where t*k is such a position or t*k >= len(word).

edge_inputs.description: k equals length, one step always suffices
highly periodic string allows early revert

stress.description: long periodic string for KMP stress
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    period = rng.randint(1, max(1, size // 10))
    unit = ''.join(rng.choice('abcd') for _ in range(period))
    word = (unit * ((size // period) + 1))[:size]
    k = rng.randint(1, max(1, period))
    return [word, k]
